dimensions.rationale: Ponvory faces a crowded oral MS formulary landscape where Mayzent holds incumbent S1P positioning at PBM level. CVS/Caremark and Express Scripts have restrictive step-therapy protocols favoring generic dimethyl fumarate and Mayzent before Ponvory. NICE approval provides a global HTA anchor, but US payer medical policy development has lagged — as predicted by the Neuroscience TA lens, access trails approval by 3-9 months and we are still seeing non-preferred tier placement at two of the top three PBMs. Kesimpta's aggressive contracting with OptumRx further compresses Ponvory's formulary shelf space. The specialty pharmacy channel is not yet optimized; three of five target SP partners have incomplete intake workflows for the dose titration pack, creating friction at the point of first fill.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Immediate: Execute payer value dossier refresh emphasizing OPTIMUM superiority data and cardiac safety differentiation vs Mayzent to secure preferred tier at CVS/Caremark and Express Scripts by Q3 2026. Negotiate volume-based rebate agreements that undercut Mayzent net pricing by 8-12%. Owner: Market Access. KPI: Preferred tier status at 2 of top 3 PBMs by 2026-09-30.

dimensions.rationale: OPTIMUM trial data showing superiority over teriflunomide on annualized relapse rate is the strongest clinical asset, but amplification has been inconsistent — only 40% of target MS KOLs have been engaged with structured OPTIMUM data presentations since launch. Cardiac safety profile education is underdeveloped; MSL teams report that neurologists still conflate Ponvory's cardiac monitoring requirements with those of fingolimod and Mayzent, undermining the S1P1 selectivity (no S1P3 activity) differentiation. MS nurse and coordinator engagement is a stated priority but lacks a formal medical education curriculum. MSL deployment coverage is adequate in top 20 MS centers but thin in mid-tier academic and community neurology practices where naive patient capture opportunity is highest.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Near-term: Deploy a targeted MSL surge to top 50 MS centers (beyond current top 20) with a structured OPTIMUM data deck and cardiac safety comparison module by 2026-06-30. Commission an independent CME series through CMSC on S1P receptor selectivity and cardiac outcomes, targeting MS nurses and coordinators. Owner: Medical Affairs. KPI: 75% KOL engagement rate by Q3 2026; 30 nurse/coordinator CME completions per month.

dimensions.rationale: Brand messaging anchored on three approved claims — OPTIMUM superiority, S1P1 selectivity/cardiac safety, and dose titration simplicity — is clinically sound but commercially underdifferentiated in neurologist perception. Competitive share-of-voice analysis (IQVIA) shows Kesimpta outspending Ponvory 2.5:1 on HCP digital channels and Ocrevus dominating conference presence. The naive patient capture priority lacks a dedicated campaign; current messaging skews toward switch patients already on oral S1P therapies. DTC investment has been minimal, which is a missed opportunity given the Neuroscience TA reality that diagnosis gaps and stigma mean DTP/DTC investment yields differently — but for MS specifically, there is an informed, activated patient population through National MS Society channels that we are underleveraging. The cardiac monitoring differentiation message is not landing because it requires clinical context that a rep detail alone cannot deliver; this is a medical-commercial integration gap.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Immediate: Launch a 'First Oral for First-Line' naive patient capture campaign with dedicated HCP and DTC components targeting newly diagnosed RMS patients. Reallocate 20% of switch-focused digital spend to naive patient digital journeys via NMS Society partnership. Develop a co-branded medical-commercial cardiac safety leave-behind validated by Medical-Legal-Regulatory. Owner: Marketing. KPI: Naive patient new-to-brand Rx share increase from current baseline by 15% within 6 months.

dimensions.rationale: Field force is deployed but neurology KOL networks are small and interconnected — reputation risk from inconsistent messaging across reps and MSLs is elevated. CRM data shows call plan adherence at 78% against target neurologists, below the 85% threshold for launch phase. Hub services team reports that dose titration pack logistics create unique fulfillment questions that the call center is not fully trained to handle, leading to 12% first-call resolution failure rate on titration-related inquiries. Incentive compensation structure overweights total Rx volume without differentiating naive starts from switches, misaligning with the stated naive patient capture priority.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Near-term: Retrain hub call center staff on titration pack logistics and establish a dedicated Ponvory titration support line. Revise IC plan for H2 2026 to weight naive patient starts at 1.5x switch Rx value. Increase call plan adherence monitoring with weekly regional reviews. Owner: Commercial Ops. KPI: Call plan adherence >85% by 2026-06-30; first-call resolution on titration inquiries >90%.

dimensions.rationale: FDA and EMA approvals are in hand with a clean label for relapsing forms of MS. No REMS requirement — this is a meaningful advantage vs. Tysabri (REMS) and vs. fingolimod's cardiac monitoring burden. Label supports all three approved key messages without stretch. However, the cardiac safety differentiation messaging ('no S1P3 activity supports cardiac safety') must be handled carefully in promotional materials — the label states the mechanism but does not make a direct comparative cardiac superiority claim vs. other S1P modulators. Promotional review committee must ensure all competitive cardiac messaging stays within labeled claims. NICE approval is unrestricted, reinforcing value positioning globally.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Strategic: Conduct a promotional material audit of all cardiac safety messaging to confirm alignment with FDA-approved labeling language. Develop a Medical-Legal-Regulatory guidance document on permissible cardiac differentiation claims for field teams. Owner: Regulatory. KPI: Zero FDA warning letters or OPDP citations related to cardiac safety claims.

dimensions.rationale: Patient assistance programs exist but are not competitively differentiated. Kesimpta's Novartis-backed patient support ecosystem (including nurse navigator, injection training, copay support) sets a high bar that Ponvory's current hub services do not match. The dose titration pack — while a clinical advantage — creates a patient education burden at initiation that the current welcome kit and digital onboarding tools do not adequately address. Adherence monitoring is passive (refill tracking only) with no proactive touchpoints during the 14-day titration period when dropout risk is highest. Financial assistance copay card program is in market but maximum benefit thresholds are lower than Kesimpta and Ocrevus equivalents, creating a price perception gap at the patient level.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Immediate: Implement a proactive patient engagement program with nurse-led check-in calls on days 3, 7, and 14 of titration to reduce early discontinuation. Increase copay card maximum annual benefit to parity with Kesimpta. Develop a digital titration companion app with daily dose reminders and symptom tracking. Owner: Patient Support / Commercial Ops. KPI: 30-day persistence rate increase from current baseline by 10 percentage points within 6 months.

dimensions.rationale: This is the #1 risk area. Ponvory occupies a strategically vulnerable middle position: less convenient than Kesimpta (monthly injection, no titration), less data depth than Ocrevus (pivotal trials in broader populations), and directly overlapping mechanism with Mayzent in the S1P class where Mayzent has incumbent formulary positioning. The cardiac selectivity differentiation is scientifically valid but has not yet translated into a meaningful prescriber preference signal — IQVIA TRx data shows Ponvory holding <8% oral MS share vs. Mayzent's 14% among S1P modulators. Biosimilar entry is not an immediate threat for Ponvory itself, but Ocrevus biosimilar development (potential 2028-2029 entry) could reshape the competitive landscape by making high-efficacy IV therapy more accessible, compressing oral therapy's total addressable market. Competitive clock: Kesimpta is aggressively pursuing guideline inclusion via AAN and is sponsoring real-world evidence studies designed to establish it as preferred first-line over oral S1P modulators.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Immediate: Commission a rapid head-to-head health economic comparison (Ponvory vs. Kesimpta, Ponvory vs. Mayzent) for payer and KOL audiences. Accelerate real-world evidence generation from claims data showing cardiac event rates on Ponvory vs. other S1P modulators. Engage AAN guideline committee members through compliant medical education to ensure Ponvory's differentiated profile is represented in upcoming guideline updates. Owner: Medical Affairs (RWE), Marketing (competitive response). KPI: Oral MS TRx share >12% by 2026-12-31.

dimensions.rationale: Oral formulation simplifies supply chain vs. biologics or CAR-T assets. Dose titration starter packs require specific packaging and distribution coordination but no cold chain. Specialty pharmacy network is contracted with five major SP partners, though three have incomplete intake workflows as noted in market access dimension. No current supply disruption signals. Inventory levels at SP partners are adequate for projected demand. However, the titration pack introduces a unique SKU management challenge — SP partners must maintain both titration packs and maintenance dose inventory, and stockout of titration packs specifically would halt new patient starts even if maintenance supply is abundant.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Near-term: Establish minimum titration pack safety stock requirements at all five SP partners with automated reorder triggers. Complete intake workflow integration at the three lagging SP partners by 2026-06-15. Owner: Supply/Distribution and Market Access. KPI: Zero titration pack stockouts at contracted SP partners; all five SP partners with fully integrated intake workflows.
